title: Introduce the sync command
body:
The skopeo sync command can sync images between a SOURCE and a destination.

The purpose of this command is to assist with the mirroring of container images from different docker registries to a single docker registry.

Right now the following transport matrix is implemented:

  * `docker://` -> `docker://`
  * `docker://` -> `dir:`
  * `dir:` -> `docker://`

The `dir:` transport is supported to handle the use case of air-gapped environments.
In this context users can perform an initial sync on a trusted machine connected to the internet; that would be a `docker://` -> `dir:` sync.
The target directory can be copied to a removable drive that can then be plugged into a node of the air-gapped environment. From there a `dir:` -> `docker://` sync will import all the images into the registry serving the air-gapped environment.

The image namespace is changed during the  `docker://` to `docker://` or `dir:` copy. The FQDN of the registry hosting the image will be added as new root namespace of the image. For example, the image `registry.example.com/busybox:latest` will be copied to `registry.local.lan/registry.example.com/busybox:latest`.

The image namespace is not changed when doing a
`dir:` -> `docker://` sync operation.

The alteration of the image namespace is used to nicely scope images coming from different registries (the Docker Hub, quay.io, gcr, other registries). That allows all of them to be hosted on the same registry without incurring in clashes and making their origin explicit.

## TODO

I hope you like this feature and the direction it's going. Once we agree on its final design we will update this PR to extend the current test suites.

## Future work

Currently sync will keep adding missing content from `SOURCE` to `DESTINATION`. It would be nice to add a `--delete` flag to remove from `DESTINATION` contents that are no longer available inside of `SOURCE. That would be a bit like rsync's `--delete` flag.

If wanted, that should be addressed with a separate PR.

Signed-off-by: Flavio Castelli <fcastelli@suse.com>
Co-authored-by: Marco Vedovati <mvedovati@suse.com>

comments.body: @mtrmac PTAL as well
@runcom @rhatdan  @mtrmac friendly ping. I'd love to see this feature in an upcoming Skopeo release.
I would like to see this a little less "docker' centric.  Perhaps use some examples other then docker.io.  registry.example.com or some such.
@mtrmac @runcom Can we merge this?
I’m afraid I still haven’t actually reviewed this. My fault entirely.

Hi guys, I am currently writing a bunch of scripts to do exactly that for our Kubernetes cluster running on an air-gapped place, then I discovered this PR 👍 ...any chance of getting this reviewed or even merged soon?

> The image namespace is changed during the docker:// to docker:// or dir: copy. The FQDN of the registry hosting the image will be added as new root namespace of the image. For example, the image 
> registry.example.com/busybox:latest will be copied to 
> registry.local.lan/registry.example.com/busybox:latest.

I would prefer to not have the original registry name as namespace in the destination registry. The source registry might be a internal registry where the name changes over time or where we want to hide the actual name on the customer side.

Maybe that is something which could be configurable?

> I would prefer to not have the original registry name as namespace in the destination registry. The
> source registry might be a internal registry where the name changes over time or where we want to 
> hide the actual name on the customer side.
>
> Maybe that is something which could be configurable?

We could add a `--dest-prefix` flag to allow something like that:

```
skopeo sync --dest-prefix acme-inc docker://wip-registry.lan/busybox docker://example.com 
```

That would copy all the `busybox` images from `wip-registry.lan/busybox` to `example.com/acme-inc/busybox`.

We should also cover this option inside of the source yaml file. Let's take this `source.yaml` as an example:

```yaml
docker.io:
    images:
        busybox: []
wip-registry:
    dest-prefix: acme-inc
    images:
        coreos/etcd:
            - latest
```

Let's assume we run this command:

```
skopeo sync --source-yaml source.yaml docker://target.com
```

By this point, inside of the `target.com` registry, we would have the following images:

  * `docker.io/busybox` -> `docker pull target.com/docker.io/busybox`
  * `acme-inc/coreos/etcd` -> `docker pull target.com/acme-inc/coreos/etcd`


Would that work with you?

How should we proceed if everybody agrees with this design? Should we get this PR merged and then implement this new flag via a new PR (to limit the amount of code to be reviewed)?
Rebased and added a few integration tests for `sync`. Run with: `make test-integration TESTFLAGS="-check.f Sync"`

@marcov, could you rebase this change on master?

@containers/image-maintainers, can we revive this PR? We're forced to run this downstream but would prefer to have a non-patched skopeo :)
@vrothberg Rebased, and also (hopefully) fixed a problem with integration tests failing when repeating the setup of a openshift cluster.
@flavio @marcov Could you rebase and update to the latest https://github.com/containers/image
@Vad1mo yes, and thanks for the ping.

The automated prefix (eg. _docker.io_) of the whole domain to the destination namespace like 
is unexpected to the user. 

```
from  = "docker://vad1mo/zportal:1.0.142"
to    = "docker://docker.8gears.com/vad1mo/docker.io/vad1mo/zportal:1.0.142"
```

IMHO, this should not be the default behavior or at least there should be a possibility to modify this behavior.

In my case I want to migrate images from one registry to another, and the namespaces should stay the same even if the domain changes.

@Vad1mo we are aware of that, we also encountered this situation in our use cases. We plan to add the option to modify this behaviour.

As a temporary workaround, you can do:
- `sync docker://vad1mo/zportal dir:localdir`
and 
- `sync dir:localdir/docker.io/ docker://docker.8gears.com/`
Rebased on master. Heads up: I realized that TLS verification is OFF by default  when source is a YAML file, I need to change the mapping key from `tls-verify` to `skip-tls` to avoid this.
@runcom @rhatdan @mtrmac friendly ping.
@mtrmac Needs your review, but I like the idea.
any updates on this? also having sync working with Bittorrent ( #540 ) would be awesome.

@luisdavim copying from Bittorrent would be trivial to support, as *sync* is copying images using `copy.Image` (same as *copy*).

As for the current status of this patch, we are waiting for any feedback from the maintainers.

@vrothberg @mtrmac @runcom Any movement on this patch.  I would like to get this into skopeo and cut a new release.

@flavio Needs a rebase for the man page change.

Rebased. 
I still need to add a couple of changes:
- turn `tls-verify` on by default when reading sources from a yaml file
- filter out not applicable src and dest flags, this is needed since flags/options processing has been unified in `utils.go`
I have addressed the changes detailed in my previous comment with a new push.
Thanks a lot, @marcov! @mtrmac, let's have a look at this PR as it's warming up again. If the approaching cold doesn't strike me, I will start tomorrow.

Thanks for starting reviewing @mtrmac!

>I also still feel very hesitant about hard-coding the use of the source name as a namespace in the destination.

We can switch this in an option (off by default) to make the behaviour of `sync` consistent with `copy`. We explained above the need for the namespace scoping when dealing with homonymous repositories on different registries.

> ```shell
> [root@nebuchadnezzar skopeo]$ ./skopeo sync docker://localhost:5000 dir:`pwd`/sync-test
> …
> FATA[0002] Error copying tag 'docker://localhost:5000': Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://localhost:5000: Error reading manifest 5000 in docker.io/library/localhost: errors:
> ```
>
> That's something we must address and something that must be clear documented in manpage and the help text. The big question is: _How do we translate source and destination without breaking the reference parsing?_

As the example above shows, the `docker://` string format, as is, just does not leave any “room” for specifying namespace prefixes; it was originally designed to support short names for `docker.io` images instead.

We _can_ write a different parser that supports prefixes (by requiring a host name to always be present — recall the earlier discussion about such a parser for `sysregistriesv2` names, although they don’t have the `docker://` prefix), but then we would end up with two different `docker://` syntaxes that have different rules.

In retrospect, accepting hostname-unqualified names in `docker://` at all was probably a mistake, but a ~necessary one at the time, and anyway here we are.

---

I don’t have a _good_ suggestion how to solve this; my best guess is not using the `alltransports.ParseImageName` format at all, and have `sync` explicitly say `--docker-source` `--dir-source` / `--docker-dest` / `--dir-dest`, or maybe `--source-type docker` / `--dest-type docker`, or something like that, and modulo all the bikeshedding about spelling and ordering and explicit options vs. positional parameters (vs. YAML); and then construct the `docker.Reference` / `dir.Reference` values with explicit knowledge of those particular transports.

Better ideas are certainly welcome.
Seems like this PR Has stalled again?
@rhatdan from my side I'll plan to progress on this next week when I'll be back from sick leave.

> We explained above the need for the namespace scoping when dealing with homonymous repositories on different registries.

(To make sure this is not lost) https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/524#discussion_r274606257 .

> I don’t have a good suggestion how to solve this; my best guess is not using the alltransports.ParseImageName format at all, and have sync explicitly say --docker-source --dir-source / --docker-dest / --dir-dest, or maybe --source-type docker / --dest-type docker, or something like that, and modulo all the bikeshedding about spelling and ordering and explicit options vs. positional parameters (vs. YAML); and then construct the docker.Reference / dir.Reference values with explicit knowledge of those particular transports. > > Better ideas are certainly welcome. I'd like to propose something different, that would allow to keep using `docker` and `dir` transports to specify source and destinations (with one exception). | Source | Destination | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | | Repository
e.g.
`docker://busybox` | Repository
e.g.
`docker://localhost:5000/busybox` | Source and destination are parsed as docker references

(both of them must be untagged)| | Repository
e.g.
`docker://busybox` | Directory
e.g.
`dir:$(pwd)/syncImages`| Source is a docker reference, destination a `dir:path`

(inside `path` there will be one directory per image tag)| | Directory
e.g.
`dir:$(pwd)/syncImages` | **Register path**
e.g.
`localhost:5000[/namespace]` | Destination is not a docker reference | ------------------ The only thing "special" here is that, when syncing from a directory, the destination is not a repository but a register path. This means the destination will not be specified with the `docker` transport prefix, and will not be parsed with `alltransports.ParseImageName`. It will be parsed as a URL (if that's ok for you), or any other way that fits better. Let me know what you think.

Hi @vrothberg, @mtrmac , 
I'd like to get a feedback on the proposal in my previous comment to advance with PR.

I am getting back to this now. Sorry for the long silence, @marcov! My goal is to avoid a birthday party for this PR, so I raise the priority on my side.

@mtrmac @rhatdan PTAL as well

Hi @vrothberg, I first wanted to know what do you think about [my proposition](#issuecomment-484800652) of avoiding the (ab)use of `docker://` transport to identify a registry URL.
Once we agree on that, I'll update the PR to include fixes to the other details.

> (inside `path` there will be one directory per image tag)
>
> | Source | Destination | Notes |
> | --- | --- | --- |
> | Directory e.g. `dir:$(pwd)/syncImages` | **Register path** e.g. `localhost:5000[/namespace]` | Destination is not a docker reference |
>
> The only thing "special" here is that, when syncing from a directory, the destination is not a repository but a register path.
> This means the destination will not be specified with the `docker` transport prefix, and will not be parsed with `alltransports.ParseImageName`. It will be parsed as a URL (if that's ok for you), or any other way that fits better.

1) It’s surprising that a source and destination for docker repositories use different syntaxes, all the more so when about every use of a docker repository as a source should eventually be parsed with use of a different repository as a destination.
2) This does not really say how the parser decides that the destination should be interpreted as a “registry path”. Is `dir:5000/foo` a “registry path”? Would this be done specifically by checking for `dir:` prefix as a special case? (if so, how does that evolve long term?) Would there be a `--destination-is-docker` option?
@mtrmac, maybe we could add an `alltransports.ParseImageNameOffline` (naming to be improved) that would not ping the registry?

`ParseImageName` does not ping anything (at least for `docker://`; it does do some FS operations on local FS paths, and various non-trivial lookups for `containers-storage`). The concern is short names vs. registry prefixes, and the inability to say “root of this namespace on a registry” using the `docker://` syntax, see your https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/524#pullrequestreview-210800367

> `ParseImageName` does not ping anything (at least for `docker://`; it does do some FS operations on local FS paths, and various non-trivial lookups for `containers-storage`). The concern is short names vs. registry prefixes, and the inability to say “root of this namespace on a registry” using the `docker://` syntax, see your [#524 (review)](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/524#pullrequestreview-210800367)

Let's say the unbearable heat impacting my concentration today, apologies. Thanks for helping me out!

Regarding the CLI, I prefer @mtrmac's proposal:

> --source-type docker / --dest-type docker [...] and then construct the docker.Reference / dir.Reference values with explicit knowledge of those particular transports.

I find it cleaner as there are no special cases which are always hard to understand and hard document. The heat is killing me but I am not sure that I could come up with a better proposal even if would snow.

FWIW I would slightly prefer `--docker-source …`/`--dir-source`/… over `--source-type` because
1) it’s one less argument to pass every time
2) it would be much easier to write completions / validation / documentation for `--docker-source` than for a generic source (when `--source-type` might not yet have been provided by the user on the command line)
3) it makes it a bit more explicit that adding supports to new transports involves new design work

OTOH it is clearly less structurally elegant, e.g. verifying that the user has specified exactly one source and exactly one destination would be more annoying.

Ultimately, this is of course the same layer of indirection however we slice it, it’s “only” how we expose that indirection to users.

Thanks for the suggestions @mtrmac, @vrothberg. Are you then implying to drop the `transport://` prefix altogether?
That's because  ` --docker-source …/--dir-source` still leaves the ambiguity of interpreting `docker://localhost:5000` as repo `localhost` with tag `5000`, instead of registry at domain `localhost` and port `5000`.

> Thanks for the suggestions @mtrmac, @vrothberg. Are you then implying to drop the `transport://` prefix altogether?
> That's because ` --docker-source …/--dir-source` still leaves the ambiguity of interpreting `docker://localhost:5000` as repo `localhost` with tag `5000`, instead of registry at domain `localhost` and port `5000`.

Right, it being a source is still an issue. For destinations we expect it to be a registry or namespace, so we have the knowledge there. This means we need a way to make the difference between an image and a registry/namespace, explicit. How can we make this explicit? I see two options:

* We add yet another CLI-flag and yaml option. Personally, I'd like to avoid bloating the sync command further.
* We add a dedicated syntax for that. `localhost:5000/*`, for instance, should be universally understood.

Sources are always repositories, so we should be fine (syncing registry to registry may be something worth adding later).

About destinations, that's correct what you say, it's either registry or registry + namespace. In both cases, we can't use `alltransports.ParseImageName`, so parsing will be done using strings tokenization.

> Thanks for the suggestions @mtrmac, @vrothberg. Are you then implying to drop the `transport://` prefix altogether?

Yes; the ~whole point is to avoid the existing fixed `docker://` semantics, so that `skopeo sync` can define some other format.

> That's because ` --docker-source …/--dir-source` still leaves the ambiguity of interpreting `docker://localhost:5000` as repo `localhost` with tag `5000`, instead of registry at domain `localhost` and port `5000`.

The way I think about it, `--docker-source`/`--docker-destination` would (both using the same semantics, so that we don’t have three!) define an input format that is a good match for the `sync` operation, probably removing the `docker.io[/library]` normalization and requiring that all input explicitly starts with a host[:port], making this unambiguous.

I force pushed a new version based on what we discussed until now. I have removed considerable parts of code (related to parsing URLs) and also simplified the logic.

I recognize it's quite a bit of code / docs to review, so I don't expect this done immediately.

Some insights:
- I introduced the `--src` and `--dest` flags that accept as values `docker`, `dir` and `yaml`.
- I introduced the `--scoped` flag, to include full source namespace when writing to destination.
- Source repository are still parsed with `alltransports.ParseImageName`, the way it works now is just fine.
- I introduced a new `dockerImageOptions` set of options, that are only applicable to the `docker` transport. In this way I don't need to filter out unneeded flags from `imageOptions`.
Is this PR in the pipeline to get merged? We have a situation where this PR can significantly help our use case.

> Is this PR in the pipeline to get merged? We have a situation where this PR can significantly help our use case.

@prashant182, at some point it will. It's a rather big PR and we're currently under load but will review asap. Happy to see the sync functionality would help your use case!

All my comments have already been stated except one: would you consider adding a short sentence or two to the man page `DESCRIPTION` summarizing the October comments in this thread, explaining why it's necessary to use `--src/--dest` options instead of the more conventional prefixes? I find those awkward, and believe that others may appreciate a little insight into the problem they solve. Thank you.

Thanks for the reviews @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @edsantiago

I've addressed your comments, they're all valid.
@mtrmac did you have a chance to review these latest changes?

Thanks for the PR and the [blog post](https://flavio.castelli.me/2018/07/18/hackweek-project-docker-registry-mirror/)! It's great and fits our use case perfectly.

Is there an ETA for merging this PR? Is it stable enough to use a custom build until it gets merged?

At least the openSUSE/SUSE registries don't have different names for every architecture, which can lead to the fact, that not every tag is available for all architectures. skopeo sync aborts in such a case. Is there any way to tell skopeo to ignore this tag in this case?

FATA[0001] Error copying tag "docker://registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/busybox:1.30.1-4.67": Error choosing an image from manifest list docker://registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/busybox:1.30.1-4.67: no image found in manifest list for architecture amd64, OS linux

> At least the openSUSE/SUSE registries don't have different names for every architecture, which can lead to the fact, that not every tag is available for all architectures. skopeo sync aborts in such a case. Is there any way to tell skopeo to ignore this tag in this case?

I guess that's something `skopeo-sync` has to do. I guess that @nalind's PR to properly support manifest lists (i.e., https://github.com/containers/image/pull/400) could help.

Yeah, I think setting `ImageListSelection: copy.CopyAllImages` in the `copy.Options` created in `syncOptions.Run` would be enough to copy tags that point to lists as lists+images, and non-lists as non-lists.  The `docker` and `dir` transports don't return errors indicating that they can't handle lists, so you shouldn't need to add any logic to decide what to do in that situation.
